parts.text: Hi, I have a wheat field at latitude 19.5 and longitude 75.3, field size is 2.5ha, planted on 2026-05-10.

Hello! Based on the weather and upcoming rainfall, you only need to irrigate on two days. On June 27, please apply 156,212.5 liters, and on June 28, apply 26,250 liters. Thanks to the expected rain, you do not need to water at all from June 29 to July 3. By following this smart schedule instead of standard baseline watering, you will save 867,500 liters of water—an impressive savings of 82.6%!

Advisory: This recommendation supplements, does not replace, local agricultural extension advice.

Hello! Based on the weather and upcoming rainfall, you only need to irrigate on two days. On June 27, please apply 156,212.5 liters, and on June 28, apply 26,250 liters. Thanks to the expected rain, you do not need to water at all from June 29 to July 3. By following this smart schedule instead of standard baseline watering, you will save 867,500 liters of water—an impressive savings of 82.6%!

Advisory: This recommendation supplements, does not replace, local agricultural extension advice.

To keep your crop healthy this week, you only need to irrigate on June 27 (apply 308,698 liters) and June 28 (apply 52,675 liters). Thanks to generous rainfall from June 29 through July 3, you can keep the irrigation off for the rest of the week. By utilizing this smart schedule instead of a rigid daily routine, you will save about 1,739,000 liters of water—a fantastic 82.8% reduction in water use!

Advisory: This recommendation supplements, does not replace, local agricultural extension advice.

Hi, I have a wheat field at latitude 10.0 and longitude 20.0, field size is 3.0ha, planted on 2026-05-10.

Here is your watering guide for June 27 to July 3. Good news: rain on June 28 means you can skip watering that day, and July 3 needs only a tiny 435 liters. For the rest of the week, apply: 118,005 liters on June 27; 55,980 liters on June 29; 104,820 liters on June 30; 145,275 liters on July 1; and 180,615 liters on July 2. This smart scheduling saves you a massive 655,200 liters of water (52% savings) compared to standard irrigation!

Advisory: This recommendation supplements, does not replace, local agricultural extension advice.
